concept.title: What is Postman?
concept.shortdesc: Learn what Postman is, why it is used, and how it simplifies working with APIs.

conbody.p: Postman is an API platform that helps developers design, test, document, monitor, and collaborate on APIs throughout the API lifecycle.
It provides a graphical interface for sending HTTP requests, inspecting responses, organizing API requests into collections, and automating common development tasks.
Although Postman is widely used by software developers, it is also valuable for testers, technical writers, DevOps engineers, product managers, and anyone who works with APIs.

section.title: Why use Postman?
section.p: Postman simplifies working with APIs by providing a single platform for designing, testing, documenting, and managing API requests.
section.note: Postman is available as both a desktop application and a web application. Most guides in this documentation use the desktop application.
